args.query: site:edd.ca.gov unemployment identity verification mailed notice documents

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Identity Verification for Unemployment

  - url: https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/identity-verification/

  - source: California Employment Development Department

  - domain: edd.ca.gov


  ## Content


  You must verify your identity when applying for unemployment benefits. EDD will
  ask for more documents if it cannot confirm your identity with the information you
  provided when you applied. This can happen whether you applied online or by phone,
  fax, or mail.


  ### ID.me verification process


  The ID.me verification process is used if you apply online. When you first apply
  for unemployment benefits through UI Online, you will be redirected to the ID.me
  site. You must provide your Social Security number, take a selfie, and upload a
  photo of your ID to verify your identity.


  If more information is needed to verify your identity, you will need to join a video
  call with ID.me. You must provide either two primary documents or one primary and
  two secondary documents. Primary documents can include a driver license, passport
  or passport card, Permanent Resident Card, Employment Authorization Card, Certificate
  of Naturalization, federal or state ID, veteran health ID card, TSA ID card, or
  Department of Homeland Security trusted traveler card. Secondary documents can include
  items such as a health insurance card, Social Security card, birth certificate,
  school documents, voter registration card, citizen ID card, military separation
  documents, or foreign birth document.


  After the ID.me video call, finish your unemployment application. Log in to UI Online
  and go back to your application. UI Online will take you to the ID.me login page.
  Log in to ID.me so your identity information can be shared with EDD. Select Allow
  and finish completing your application. If you cannot verify your identity through
  ID.me online, you will need to apply for unemployment benefits by phone, fax, or
  mail.


  ### Requests for additional identity verification


  If EDD could not verify your identity based on the information you gave when you
  applied, EDD will notify you that you need to provide additional documents. EDD
  will also provide the specific method you must use to verify your identity. You
  may need to complete the request in UI Online or respond to the Request for Identity
  Verification (DE 1326C) mailed to you.


  If you are required to use UI Online, visit your UI Online homepage and select Upload
  Identity Documents. You will only see this option if there has been an issue verifying
  your identity.


  For EDD to verify your identity, you must provide two documents: one photo identification
  document and one other identity document. The photo identification must show your
  name, date of birth, and photograph.


  ### Submission deadline and penalties


  EDD must receive ID documents within 10 calendar days of the Mail Date on the Request
  for Identity Verification (DE 1326C). If you need more time, you must call 1-866-401-2849
  or mail a request to the address at the top of the request form. EDD must receive
  the extension request within 10 calendar days of the Mail Date.


  If EDD does not receive correct identification documents or a request for more time,
  or cannot verify your identity, you will not be eligible for benefits, you will
  receive a disqualification notice by mail, and you will need to file an appeal to
  get benefits.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Identity Verification for Unemployment
url: https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/identity-verification/

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Step 5: Certify for Benefits\n\
  - url: https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/step-5-certify-for-benefits\n- source:\
  \ California Employment Development Department\n- domain: edd.ca.gov\n\n## Content\n\
  \nYou already applied for unemployment benefits, but you need to answer a few more\
  \ questions before you can receive benefit payments. Certifying is the process of\
  \ answering basic questions every two weeks to tell the Employment Development Department\
  \ that you are still unemployed and eligible to receive benefit payments. You first\
  \ certify for benefits about two weeks after EDD processes your application and\
  \ again every two weeks after that.\n\nEDD will send an email or contact you by\
  \ mail when it is time to certify. If you fail to certify accurately or on time,\
  \ it will delay or prevent payment.\n\nThe fastest way to certify is online through\
  \ myEDD. You can also certify by telephone or by mail, but payments may take longer\
  \ to process. The certification questions can be confusing, so read them carefully\
  \ and review the certification question information if needed. Report any work and\
  \ wages, even if you were not paid yet.\n\n### Certify with myEDD\n\nmyEDD is the\
  \ fastest way to certify for benefits and report work and wages. To get started,\
  \ log in to myEDD and select UI Online. If you have wages to report, select Report\
  \ Wages to use the Weekly Wage Reporting Tool. The information you enter can be\
  \ used in your certification.\n\nCheck the Notifications section of the UI Online\
  \ homepage. If weeks are available, select Certify for Benefits and answer all questions.\
  \ If no weeks are available, the system will tell you when to check again and will\
  \ send an email reminder.\n\nIn UI Online, use the Help feature for more instructions.\n\
  \n### Certify by Phone\n\nEDD Tele-Cert is a way to certify for unemployment benefits\
  \ by phone. Call the Unemployment Automated Self-Service Line at 1-866-333-4606,\
  \ select Menu Option 1, enter your four-digit PIN, answer the certification questions,\
  \ and confirm your answers.\n\nIf you need to report work and wages, a change of\
  \ address, or a phone number, certify through myEDD or by mail. If you submit late,\
  \ you may be scheduled for a phone interview or receive an electronic determination,\
  \ and benefits could be denied.\n\n### Certify by Mail\n\nIf you certify by mail,\
  \ complete, sign, and mail the Continued Claim Form (DE 4581CTO) every two weeks.\
  \ Submit the form within 14 days of the \u201CComplete and Mail This Form On\u201D\
  \ date above Question 1 on the form. If you submit early by mail, your payment may\
  \ be delayed and EDD will send you a new form.\n"

title: 'Step 5: Certify for Benefits'

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Reopen an Unemployment Insurance Claim

  - url: https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/reopen-a-claim/

  - source: California Employment Development Department

  - domain: edd.ca.gov


  ## Content


  If it has been more than 30 days since you last certified for benefits, your unemployment
  claim will become inactive. To continue receiving benefits, you must reopen your
  claim.


  You can reopen your claim if it was filed within the last 52 weeks and you have
  not used all of your benefits. If your benefit year ended, you may need to reapply
  for unemployment. EDD provides separate information for benefit year end situations.


  Waiting to reopen or apply for a new claim can delay benefit payments.


  ### How to reopen a claim online


  The fastest and most convenient way to reopen your claim is through myEDD. You can
  reopen your claim at any time by following these steps:


  1. Access your online account. Log in to myEDD and select UI Online.

  2. Select Reopen Your Claim from the Notifications section of your UI Online homepage.
  This link will only appear if you need to reopen your claim.

  3. Check your status. Access your UI Online homepage to check the status of your
  request.


  If your claim was successfully reopened, you will see a confirmation message in
  UI Online. EDD will also let you know when to certify for benefits by mailing the
  paper certification or by sending a message to your UI Online account. If more information
  is needed, EDD will schedule a phone interview or email a questionnaire. The phone
  interview date and time will be sent by mail and will also appear in the Notifications
  section of your UI Online homepage. It may take up to 10 days to reopen your claim
  if EDD needs to review it.


  ### Reopen by phone, fax, or mail


  You can call EDD by phone during business hours. Interpreter services are available
  free of charge.


  You can also reopen your claim by using the paper Unemployment Insurance Application
  (DE 1101i). For faster and more secure processing, fax the completed application
  to the number listed on the form. If you mail the application, use the address on
  the form and allow additional time for processing.


  ### After you reopen your claim


  You will receive important information about your claim by mail. Read and respond
  to all requests to avoid payment delays.


  You must certify for benefits every two weeks. If you opted out of receiving the
  paper Continued Claim Form (DE 4581), you will not receive a paper form in the mail.
  Instead, you will receive an email notification when it is time to submit certifications
  through UI Online.

  '

args.reason: The active claim is already certified for the payment period and has no current certification available. The payment is held by a separate identity-verification review awaiting user action. EDD's identity-verification page says DE 1326C requests must be answered using the method specified by EDD and that documents or an extension request must be received within 10 calendar days of the mail date. The notice was mailed 2026-07-08, is due 2026-07-18, and this account has no online upload option, so the mailed notice instructions must be followed. The claim is active/open, so reopening is not indicated.

content.content:
Your payment is pending because it is **being held for identity verification**, not because you need to certify again or reopen your claim.

### What to do now

1. **Find the mailed EDD notice “Request for Identity Verification (DE 1326C).”**
2. Follow the **response method and document instructions printed on that notice**. Your account does **not** currently show an online upload option, so don’t assume you can submit through UI Online.
3. EDD’s official instructions generally require:
   - One photo ID showing your name, date of birth, and photograph, and
   - One additional identity document.
4. EDD must receive the documents—or a request for more time—within **10 calendar days of the notice’s mail date**. Your notice was mailed **July 8, 2026**, with a response deadline of **July 18, 2026**.
5. Keep copies of everything you send and use a trackable mailing method if the notice directs you to mail documents.

Your claim is active and already certified for the payment week ending **July 4, 2026**, so **certifying again will not resolve this payment**, and reopening the claim is not currently indicated.

If you need an extension, EDD’s identity-verification page says to call **1-866-401-2849** or mail the request to the address at the top of the DE 1326C notice, within the applicable deadline.

Official EDD information:
- [Identity Verification for Unemployment](https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/identity-verification/)
- [Certify for Benefits](https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/step-5-certify-for-benefits)
- [Reopen an Unemployment Insurance Claim](https://edd.ca.gov/en/unemployment/reopen-a-claim/)
